## Pull Request Description

<!-- Describe what this PR does in plain English -->

### Type of Change
- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that causes existing programs to behave differently)
- [ ] 📚 Documentation update
- [ ] 🔧 Build / installer / tooling change
- [ ] 🎨 Code style / refactor (no functional change)

### Related Issue
Closes # (issue number)

### Changes Made
<!-- List the specific files and what changed -->
- `src/`: 
- `examples/`:
- Other:

### Testing
<!-- Describe how you tested your changes -->

- [ ] I ran the existing examples: `seng examples/hello.se` etc.
- [ ] I added a new example demonstrating the change
- [ ] I tested on Windows 10 / 11
- [ ] I compiled with GCC
- [ ] I compiled with Clang

### Example seng Code (if applicable)
```seng
# Show an example that demonstrates your change
```

### Checklist
- [ ] My code follows the existing style in the codebase
- [ ] I have updated the README if my change affects the language syntax
- [ ] I have not introduced any memory leaks (check with valgrind or Dr. Memory)
- [ ] All existing examples still run correctly
- [ ] I have read the CONTRIBUTING guide
